threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think you can rely on the Tube to get anywhere on time. Good luck with that during a strike!
Last night on the 196, a guy serenaded us with Coldplay as we crawled past the Ritzy. Only in Brixton do you get a live gig for the fare.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The day I saw a kid riding a bicycle with a cat in the front basket, I realized Neukölln had seeped into my soul.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin do people calmly smoke outside train stations while waiting for delayed buses during a strike, as if it’s all part of the daily routine.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local by their hasty hop over those manhole covers—only tourists stand still to snap a pic of the street art nearby.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a Muni bus today right as a guy in a superhero cape ran to catch it. He missed, but the driver stopped to let him on anyway.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists always think paella is a staple here. But in Gràcia, you’re more likely to find a plate of ‘bomba’ than a seafood dish.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You’ll see them still clutching that paper map, staring bewilderedly at the metro signs while locals zip past, earbuds in, no second glances.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still avoid the pavement by the bus stop during rush hour—locals know that’s prime dodging territory.
Caught a night bus from Brixton last night; saw a group of blokes trying to fit a massive inflatable giraffe through the doors. Classic South London chaos.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I knew I was home when I saw a group of kids trading Pokémon cards next to a fried chicken shop, all while an elderly lady fed the pigeons nearby.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You ever notice how Kreuzberg’s street art seems to change overnight? One day a wall's blank, next it’s a canvas of protest or humor. Keeps you on your toes!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s a local when they can name at least three taco trucks that aren't La Taqueria and still get mad when Muni's late.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a dog chilling on the tracks at Civic Center today while Muni just sat there. The owner was more worried about the pup than the bus.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think the fountains are just for pretty pictures, but locals use them for a quick splash on hot summer days. That’s real life here.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by their hesitation at crossing the street during siesta—still not used to the quiet hours when everyone’s dozing off.
